evals.prompt: Given a meeting subagent processes the file `2026-03-18 Pricing Strategy.md` for Aritzia with a Gong transcript, verify it returns all required extraction fields in the correct format.
evals.expected_output: Subagent returns structured extraction with all required fields between ---BEGIN EXTRACTION--- and ---END EXTRACTION--- markers
expectations: Output contains ---BEGIN EXTRACTION--- and ---END EXTRACTION--- delimiters
MEETING_FILE field matches the input filename
DATE field is in YYYY-MM-DD format

MEETING_TYPE is Title Case and one of the allowed values (Discovery, Demo, Technical Deep-Dive, POV Kickoff, POV Check-in, Implementation, Troubleshooting, Renewal, Expansion, CSM Check-in, Internal Sync, Procurement, Pricing, Partnership, Outreach)
SUGGESTED_TITLE is either a 1-3 word Title Case phrase or 'unchanged'
ATTENDEES is a comma-separated list of full names (not first-name-only)
SUMMARY is 2-4 sentences covering what was discussed, key learnings, and decisions/next steps
LEDGER_ENTRY follows format 'M/D GY: {15-25 word summary}. Next call: M/D {type}' or 'Next call: TBD'
LEDGER_ENTRY uses M/D format (no leading zeros, no year for current year)
All MEDDPICC fields present: METRICS, ECONOMIC_BUYER, DECISION_CRITERIA, DECISION_PROCESS, PAPER_PROCESS, IDENTIFIED_PAIN, CHAMPION, COMPETITION
All CoM fields present: REQUIRED_CAPABILITIES, BUSINESS_VALUE, POSITIVE_OUTCOMES, NEGATIVE_CONSEQUENCES, BEFORE_AFTER
All TECHMAPS fields present: TECH_REQUIREMENTS, ENVIRONMENT, TECH_COMPETITORS, HERO, TECH_METRICS, ALIGNMENT, TECH_VALIDATION_PLAN, SUPPORT
TECH_STACK field present with categories (Languages, Frameworks, Cloud, etc.)
ARCHITECTURE_NOTES field present
NEW_COMPETITORS field present (value or 'none')
NEW_OBJECTIONS field present (value or 'none')
FEATURE_REQUESTS field present (value or 'none')
Fields with no relevant info contain 'none' (not empty or N/A)
Competitor names use canonical spellings from config (e.g., 'Kameleoon' not 'Chameleon')
Product names use canonical names from config (e.g., 'Guarded Releases' not 'guarded releases')
## Tasks section is NOT modified or populated in any way

evals.prompt: Given a meeting file for Arlo where the transcript refers to attendees as 'Nick', 'Arun', and 'Cooper', and the contacts/ folder has files 'Nick Meshes.md', 'Arun Kumar.md', and 'Cooper Marcus.md', verify name resolution logic.
evals.expected_output: Subagent resolves first-name-only references to full names from existing contact files and account frontmatter
expectations: First-name 'Nick' resolved to 'Nick Meshes' using existing contact file match
First-name 'Arun' resolved to 'Arun Kumar' using existing contact file match
First-name 'Cooper' resolved to 'Cooper Marcus' using existing contact file match
LD team members (SE, AE, CSM from frontmatter) resolved to full names
Attendees array uses wiki-link format: '[[Nick Meshes]]' not 'Nick Meshes'
Plain text attendee names converted to wiki-link format
Known contact names list passed to subagent prompt includes all contacts from contacts/ folder
Transcript misspellings corrected to match existing contact file spellings (e.g., phonetically similar names prefer contact file spelling)
Names that cannot be resolved to full names are kept as-is but flagged in extraction output
No-show attendees (invited but not on the call per transcript) are removed from attendees array

evals.prompt: Given three meeting subagent results for Pluralsight — a Discovery call mentioning 'VP Eng as budget holder', a Demo mentioning 'must integrate with Datadog', and a POV Kickoff mentioning 'Split.io evaluated previously' — verify MEDDPICC merge produces correct aggregated output.
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 merges MEDDPICC data from all three meetings into a single coherent account file with non-duplicated, aggregated information
expectations: MEDDPICC section has a single > [!summary] Summary callout (NOT collapsed with -)
Summary callout contains ALL eight MEDDPICC fields as one-line bullet points
Economic Buyer field includes 'VP Eng' info from Discovery meeting
Decision Criteria field includes 'Datadog integration' from Demo meeting
Competition field includes 'Split.io' from POV Kickoff meeting
Detailed ### subsections exist below the summary callout for each field with expanded info
Data from multiple meetings is aggregated, not overwritten (earlier meeting data preserved alongside newer)
Command of the Message section also aggregated from all three meetings
TECHMAPS section also aggregated from all three meetings
Section order is correct: Ledger > Open Tasks > Stakeholders > Meetings > Business Context > MEDDPICC > CoM > TECHMAPS > Tech Stack > Architecture > Salesforce Updates
No individual collapsed callouts like > [!summary]- Metrics (incorrect format)
No blank line between heading and first bullet
No blank line between last bullet and next heading

evals.prompt: Given a Rula account with meetings at Stage 2 in Salesforce, MEDDPICC showing pain validated and EB identified but no POV started, verify CEP Stage Analysis identifies correct stage.
evals.expected_output: CEP Stage Analysis recommends Stage 2 (Business Value) since POV has not started, regardless of SF stage
expectations: CEP Stage Analysis section positioned ABOVE ## MEDDPICC in the account file
Format: **Recommended: Stage {N} - {Name}** | SF: Stage {N} - {Name} | {date}
Tech Validation line present with Type and Status values
Recommended stage is the HIGHEST stage where ALL prior exit criteria are met
Stage 1 exit criteria checked: custom demo delivered, future-state alignment, next step in SFDC
Stage 2 exit criteria checked: EB POV Go/No-Go meeting, business justification confirmed, POV planned/timed
Deal NOT placed in Stage 3 without an active POV (POV gate enforced)
If SF shows Stage 3+ but no POV started and not skipped, flagged as overstaged
Key Risks section appears FIRST (before Completed and Not yet completed)
Bullet points are short: 5-10 words max, using S1/S2 shorthand
4-6 completed items listed
3-5 not-yet-completed items listed
Tech Validation Type uses allowed values (Deep Dive Demo, Guided POV, Self-Guided Trial, Skipped, Workshop, Stalled)
Tech Validation Status uses allowed values (Planning, In-Progress, Extended, Completed)

evals.prompt: Given a Samsara account where 3 meeting subagent results return attendees 'Cirpo Cinelli, Ben Harling, Shelby Belak, Sara Pion, New Person, Another Contact', and contacts/ folder already has 'Cirpo Cinelli.md' and 'Ben Harling.md' but not the others, verify contact reconciliation.
evals.expected_output: Existing contacts matched, new contacts created with correct template, enrichment subagents launched for contacts missing role or LinkedIn
expectations: Phase 4a runs BEFORE Phase 3 account updates
All attendees collected from ALL subagent ATTENDEES fields (union across meetings)
Cirpo Cinelli matched to existing contact file — no new file created
Ben Harling matched to existing contact file — no new file created
Shelby Belak: new contact file created at contacts/Shelby Belak.md
Sara Pion: new contact file created at contacts/Sara Pion.md
New Person: new contact file created at contacts/New Person.md
Another Contact: new contact file created at contacts/Another Contact.md
New contact files have correct frontmatter: name, company, role, influence, email, linkedin, notes
Company field defaults to account name (Samsara) for customer contacts
Company field set to 'LaunchDarkly' for LD team members (SE, AE, CSM)
Contact template includes ## Meeting History section with dataview query
Dataview query FROM path matches the account's meetings folder
Enrichment subagents (Phase 4b) launched IN PARALLEL with Phase 3 account updates
Enrichment subagents use haiku model by default
Enrichment subagents only launched for contacts with empty role: or linkedin: fields
Existing contacts with empty company: field get company filled in
Reconciliation table output shows each attendee, whether file existed, and action taken
Full contact roster table output shows Name, Role, LinkedIn, Status for all contacts

evals.prompt: Given a BILL account with Ledger.md containing entries for 3/13 and 3/10, and two new meeting subagent results for 3/16 and 3/13, verify ledger deduplication logic.
evals.expected_output: Only the 3/16 meeting gets a new ledger entry; the 3/13 entry is skipped because it already exists in the ledger
expectations: Existing ledger entries read during Phase 1 Discovery (Step 4)
3/13 meeting recognized as already having a ledger entry — no duplicate created
3/16 meeting gets a new ledger entry added
New entry placed at TOP of ledger (most recent first)
Entry format: '- 3/16 GY: {concise 15-25 word summary}. Next call: M/D {type}' or 'Next call: TBD'
Date format uses M/D (no leading zeros, no year for current year)
Dates from prior year use M/D/YY format (e.g., 11/3/25)
Every entry ends with 'Next call:' clause
Entries are concise — no attendee names, product descriptions, or technical details
Non-meeting content in ledger (analysis, pain points) moved to MEDDPICC section
(status) entries from /ld-weekly are removed when real meeting entries supersede them
Ledger contains ONLY meeting entries — no prose or analysis

evals.prompt: Given an Aritzia account with salesforce_opportunity and salesforce_account fields in frontmatter, and salesforce_configured: true in config, verify SF deal context pull during Phase 1.5.
evals.expected_output: SF deal context subagent launches in parallel with meeting subagents, reads Scan Open Mode steps 2O-5O, writes results to temp file for Phase 3 merge
expectations: SF deal context subagent launches in the SAME message as meeting subagents and business context subagent
Subagent reads Scan Open Mode section of /ld-salesforce SKILL.md
Subagent executes Steps 2O through 5O for the account
Results written to temp file (e.g., /tmp/sf_context_aritzia.json) not directly to account file
Phase 3 merges SF context from temp file into account frontmatter
Frontmatter salesforce_opportunity URLs updated if opportunity data changed
If account has NO salesforce_opportunity fields, SF subagent is silently skipped
If salesforce_configured is not true in config, SF subagent is silently skipped
If SF token retrieval fails, warning logged but summarization continues (not blocked)
Legacy frontmatter migration: salesforce_url renamed to salesforce_opportunity if present
Auto-correct: /Account/ URL in salesforce_opportunity field moved to salesforce_account
Auto-correct: /Opportunity/ URL in salesforce_account field moved to salesforce_opportunity
Swapped URLs logged in output summary

evals.prompt: Given a Vail Resorts account with an existing ## Business Context section containing 2 news items from January, and the business context subagent returns 3 items (1 duplicate from January, 2 new from March), verify merge logic.
evals.expected_output: Two new March headlines added at top of Recent News, January duplicate not duplicated, existing content preserved
expectations: Business context subagent launches in parallel with SF pull and meeting subagents (Phase 1.5)
Subagent searches for '{Account} company' and '{Account} news {current year}'
Existing Business Context section preserved (not overwritten)
New March headlines added at TOP of Recent News list
Duplicate January headline NOT added again
News format: '- **M/DD/YYYY**: [Headline summary](URL)'
Only news items with direct article URLs included (not Crunchbase/ZoomInfo/Tracxn profile pages)
No generic newsroom landing page URLs
Company description bullets in About section preserved if already present
If Business Context section did not exist, it would be created between ## Meetings and ## MEDDPICC
Business context results come from Phase 1.5 subagent, not a fresh web search in Phase 3

evals.prompt: Given a JFrog account with 5 meetings (3 already summarized with content in ## Summary, 2 with empty ## Summary sections), verify the skip-already-summarized logic.
evals.expected_output: Only 2 subagents launched for unsummarized meetings; 3 summarized meetings skipped entirely
expectations: First ~30 lines of each meeting file read to check ## Summary content
Meetings with non-empty ## Summary section are identified as already summarized
Meetings with empty or missing ## Summary section are identified as unsummarized
Subagents launched ONLY for the 2 unsummarized meetings
No subagent launched for the 3 already-summarized meetings
All meeting subagents for unsummarized files launched in parallel (single message)
If ALL meetings were already summarized, Phase 2 is skipped entirely and proceeds to Phase 3
Phase 3 account updates still run even if no new meetings processed (ledger, MEDDPICC can update from other sources)
Model selection checked before launching: if meeting-summary has >30% failure rate on current model, escalate to next tier

evals.prompt: Given a Procore account with a meeting file named '2026-03-20 Call.md' and the subagent returns SUGGESTED_TITLE: 'Release Guardian Demo', verify file rename logic.
evals.expected_output: File renamed from 'Call' to 'Release Guardian Demo', all references in daily notes and ledger updated
expectations: File renamed from '2026-03-20 Call.md' to '2026-03-20 Release Guardian Demo.md' via mv command
Daily notes searched around the meeting date for references to old filename
All occurrences of old link text replaced with new link text in daily notes
Wiki-link paths updated: [[LaunchDarkly/Accounts/Procore/meetings/2026-03-20 Call|...]] becomes [[LaunchDarkly/Accounts/Procore/meetings/2026-03-20 Release Guardian Demo|...]]
Display text in wiki-links also updated
Ledger references to old filename updated
Account file references to old filename updated
Only files with generic topics (Call, Check-in, Meeting, Sync) are renamed
Files with already-descriptive topics are NOT renamed even if subagent suggests something
SUGGESTED_TITLE 'unchanged' means no rename action taken

evals.prompt: Given a Writer account with SF Stage 3 but no POV started (still in POV scoping), verify CEP stage analysis flags overstaging.
evals.expected_output: CEP analysis recommends Stage 2, flags deal as overstaged since Stage 3 requires active POV
expectations: Deal evaluated against all CEP stage exit criteria sequentially
Stage 1 exit criteria verified: custom demo delivered, future-state alignment confirmed
Stage 2 exit criteria checked: EB Go/No-Go, business justification, POV planned
POV gate enforced: deal CANNOT be Stage 3 without active POV
POV scoping/planning status recognized as Stage 2 (not Stage 3)
Assessment explicitly states: 'Deal is overstaged: Stage 3 requires an active POV'
Key Risks section highlights the overstaging as a critical risk
Tech Validation Status set to 'Planning' (not 'In-Progress') since POV not started
Recommended stage is Stage 2 despite SF showing Stage 3
If deal had skipped POV entirely (Skipped type), Stage 3 bypass would be valid

evals.prompt: Given an Arlo account with an existing contact 'Nick Meshes.md' that has empty company: field and another contact 'Rod Alipour.md' with company: 'Arlo', verify existing contact update logic.
evals.expected_output: Nick Meshes' empty company field filled in based on context; Rod Alipour's existing company field left unchanged
expectations: After creating missing contacts, ALL existing contact files scanned
Nick Meshes' empty company: field detected
Company inferred from meeting transcripts and context clues
If Nick is an account contact, company set to 'Arlo'
If Nick is a LD team member, company set to 'LaunchDarkly'
If Nick is a third party (consultant, vendor), company inferred from context
Rod Alipour's existing company: 'Arlo' NOT overwritten
Enrichment subagent launched for contacts with empty role: or linkedin: fields
Enrichment subagent uses haiku model by default unless failure rate >50%
Enrichment results do NOT overwrite existing non-empty values

evals.prompt: Run /ld-summarize-account for Sweetgreen, an account with 4 unsummarized meetings, no existing MEDDPICC content, no Salesforce opportunity link, and 2 existing contacts. Verify full end-to-end execution.
evals.expected_output: Complete end-to-end processing: meetings summarized, ledger populated, MEDDPICC/CoM/TECHMAPS built from scratch, contacts reconciled, daily note updated, self-improvement logged
expectations: Phase 1: Account directory found, account file read, Ledger.md read, meetings/ listed, contacts/ listed
Phase 1: Legacy frontmatter migration checked (salesforce_url → salesforce_opportunity)
Phase 1: Salesforce URL auto-correction checked (Account vs Opportunity URLs)
Phase 1.5: SF subagent SKIPPED silently (no salesforce_opportunity in frontmatter)
Phase 1.5: Business context subagent launched for company info and news
Phase 2: All 4 meeting subagents launched in parallel in SAME message as business context subagent
Phase 2: Model selection checked (meeting-summary failure rate on current model)
Phase 2: Each subagent returns structured extraction with all fields
Phase 4a: Attendees collected from all 4 extractions, compared against 2 existing contacts
Phase 4a: Missing contacts created with correct template before Phase 3 starts
Phase 4b + Phase 3: Contact enrichment subagents launched IN PARALLEL with account file updates
Phase 3a: Generic meeting filenames renamed if subagent suggested better title
Phase 3b: Next call info updated in frontmatter from most recent meeting
Phase 3c: 4 new ledger entries added (most recent at top), each ending with 'Next call:' clause
Phase 3d: Business Context section created with About and Recent News from subagent results
Phase 3e: MEDDPICC section created from scratch with single summary callout + detailed subsections
Phase 3e: Command of the Message section created from extracted data
Phase 3e: TECHMAPS section created from extracted data
Phase 3e: Tech Stack section created with relevant categories only
Phase 3f: Architecture diagram created as ASCII art
Phase 3g: Salesforce Updates section created with correct format
Phase 4c: CEP Stage Analysis added above MEDDPICC after Phase 3 completes
Phase 5: Daily note found, /ld-summarize-account checkbox marked [x] with completion timestamp
Phase 6a: Model performance logged to ld-config.md (success/failure counts)
Phase 6a: New competitors, objections, tech patterns, feature requests logged under Discovered Patterns
Phase 6b: Template drift detection (comparison of before/after if re-run)

Output includes: meetings updated, ledger additions, business context updates, MEDDPICC fields, TECHMAPS fields, CoM fields, architecture, SF Updates, contacts created, contact roster table, next call info, daily note confirmation, learnings logged

evals.prompt: Given a Pluralsight account where the Salesforce Updates section already exists with a 3/10 latest entry and deal history, and a new 3/17 meeting is processed, verify the SF Updates section restructure.
evals.expected_output: 3/17 entry becomes the new latest entry at top, 3/10 entry moves to DEAL HISTORY, all older entries preserved
expectations: Salesforce Updates section exists inside triple-backtick code block
3/17 entry placed as the SOLE latest entry at top (above MEDDPICC block)
Previous 3/10 latest entry moved to top of DEAL HISTORY section
All existing DEAL HISTORY entries preserved below the moved 3/10 entry
DEAL HISTORY ordered newest first
No duplication: 3/17 does NOT appear in DEAL HISTORY
MEDDPICC block has fields on separate lines: '- Field: value'
TECHMAPS block has fields on separate lines: '- Field: value'
TECH STACK is one comma-separated line
Date format: M/D (no leading zeros) for current year, M/D/YY for prior years
Deal history entries are concise (~15-25 words each)
All entries from GY's perspective

evals.prompt: Given a BILL account where a meeting transcript mentions 'Statsig' (spelled as 'Stats Sig' in transcript) and 'Amplitude' (not in competitors config), verify competitor handling.
evals.expected_output: Stats Sig corrected to canonical spelling 'Statsig', Amplitude auto-added to ld-config.md competitors list
expectations: Transcript misspelling 'Stats Sig' recognized as variant of 'Statsig' from config misspellings array
Canonical spelling 'Statsig' used in all output (COMPETITION field, account file)
Amplitude identified as a competitor NOT in the config
Amplitude added to competitors array in ~/.claude/skills/ld-config.md
New competitor entry includes name, brief description inferred from context, and observed misspellings
NEW_COMPETITORS extraction field reports 'Amplitude' with context
New competitor also logged under Discovered Patterns > Pending Review in config
Product names also corrected to canonical form from config (e.g., 'feature flags' → 'Feature Flags')

evals.prompt: Given a Capital Group account with 10 meetings that all have zero content (no notes, no transcript, no external summary), verify the skill handles empty meetings gracefully.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects that all meetings lack processable content, skips subagent launch, still updates account from other sources
expectations: Each meeting file checked for ## Summary content in first ~30 lines
Meetings with zero content (no Notes, no Transcript, no External Summary) detected
No subagents launched for meetings with no processable content
If ALL meetings are empty/already summarized, Phase 2 subagents skipped entirely
Phase 3 still runs to update account from Ledger, SF context, business context, and other sources
Issue logged under Recurring Issues in ld-config.md noting the empty meeting files
Business context subagent still launches (independent of meeting content)
SF deal context subagent still launches if salesforce_opportunity exists

evals.prompt: Given a Tipalti account being processed and ld-config.md shows contact-enrichment has >50% failure rate on haiku, verify adaptive model selection escalation.
evals.expected_output: Contact enrichment subagents use sonnet instead of haiku due to high failure rate
expectations: ld-config.md Model Performance table read before launching subagents
contact-enrichment failure rate calculated from success/failure counts
If >50% failure rate on haiku, sonnet model selected for contact enrichment subagents
If meeting-summary has >30% failure rate on current model, next tier model selected
Model selection decision logged in run output
Phase 6a updates Model Performance table with new success/failure counts for the model actually used
Default model for meeting subagents is sonnet
Default model for contact enrichment is haiku

evals.prompt: Given an account file for Aya Healthcare where salesforce_opportunity contains an /Account/ URL and salesforce_account is empty, verify the auto-correct swap logic.
evals.expected_output: The /Account/ URL moved from salesforce_opportunity to salesforce_account, salesforce_opportunity cleared, swap logged
expectations: All salesforce_* frontmatter fields checked during Phase 1 Discovery
salesforce_opportunity field containing /Account/ URL detected as misplaced
URL moved to salesforce_account field (which was empty)
salesforce_opportunity field cleared after the move
Reverse case also handled: /Opportunity/ URL in salesforce_account moved to salesforce_opportunity
Swap only happens if the target field is empty or missing (no accidental overwrite)
Swap logged in output summary so user is aware of the correction
salesforce_opportunity_* (numbered) fields also checked for misplaced URLs

evals.prompt: Given an RingCentral account where the user previously manually reformatted the MEDDPICC section (reworded summaries, reordered fields), verify template drift detection in Phase 6b.
evals.expected_output: Template drift detected: MEDDPICC section was manually edited; logged under Template Feedback in ld-config.md
expectations: Account file re-read after Phase 3 writes complete
Content compared against expected template patterns
Manually reworded MEDDPICC fields detected as drift
Reordered fields detected as drift
Drift logged under ## Template Feedback in ld-config.md
Log entry includes: section name (MEDDPICC), type of change (rewording/restructuring), account name (RingCentral)
Other drift types also detected: summary style changes, section reordering, content removal/addition
Template drift detection only runs on re-runs (not first-time account processing)

evals.prompt: Given an Auditboard account where all meetings are already summarized and the ledger has a (status) entry from /ld-weekly, verify the status entry cleanup.
evals.expected_output: All meetings skipped (already summarized), (status) entry removed from ledger, account still updated from existing data
expectations: All meetings recognized as already summarized (## Summary has content)
No meeting subagents launched
Phase 3 still executes to refresh account data
Ledger (status) entry identified and removed
(status) entries are superseded by real meeting-based entries
Remaining ledger entries preserved with correct ordering (newest first)
MEDDPICC, CoM, TECHMAPS sections still updated if new information available from SF pull or business context
Daily note checkbox still marked complete
